title: gastrolobium vestitum
intro: Gastrolobium vestitum, also known as the poison or Stink Bean, is a highly toxic plant native to Western Australia, with seeds containing a powerful neurotoxin called gyromitrin. Traditionally, the seeds of this plant were used by Indigenous Australians for medicinal purposes, particularly to treat various health issues, including fever and rheumatism. The seeds contain medicinal properties that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. The seeds can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, but due to its toxicity, it is essential to handle and consume them with extreme caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
